description_long: This is a striking blue flower with six broad, pointed petals radiating symmetrically from a central point, each petal displaying a gradient from deep blue at the base to lighter blue at the tips, with subtle veining patterns. The flower's center features several slender, purple-tipped stamens that add a delicate contrast to the blue petals. The petals are slightly curved and overlap at their bases, creating a layered, whorled appearance. A slender, green stem supports the flower, with two elongated, lanceolate leaves extending outward at different angles. The overall structure is elegant and organic, with a naturalistic color transition and soft, semi-glossy surface. This flower would be visually striking in an urban park, garden, or streetscape planter.
description: A vibrant blue flower with six pointed petals, purple stamens, and a slender green stem with lanceolate leaves. The petals are symmetrically arranged and display a gradient from deep to light blue.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from a side angle, highlighting the curvature and layering of the petals, as well as the arching stem and one visible leaf.
description_view_1: This view shows the flower from a slightly rear angle, emphasizing the back of the petals, the stem, and both leaves extending outward.
description_view_2: A top-down view reveals the full radial symmetry of the six petals and the central cluster of purple stamens.
description_view_3: An angled perspective displays the overlapping petals and the way they curve outward, with the stem and leaves partially visible beneath.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with six distinct petals radiating symmetrically from the center. The petals overlap slightly at their bases, forming a regular, star-like pattern typical of many monocot flowers.
